title: PostPilot
tagline: Schedule, draft, and manage social content without the platform overhead.
description: PostPilot is a social content scheduling tool built for solo creators and small teams who need drafting, scheduling, and basic analytics without the complexity or cost of enterprise tools. Connect accounts, write posts, schedule them, review performance.
story: Built for the gap between "just post manually" and "pay $99/month for a platform you use 10% of."
journey: PostPilot started from a personal frustration: the scheduling tools that existed were either too simple (no drafts, no analytics) or too expensive for solo use. Building it was a good exercise in OAuth flows, queue management, and designing around the real constraints of social platform APIs — rate limits, token refresh, and platform-specific post formats all had to be handled properly.
forWho: Solo creators, small teams, and indie makers who need content scheduling without enterprise pricing.

detailedSpecs.architecture: Next.js app router. PostgreSQL for post and account storage. Bull queue for scheduled post dispatch. OAuth for platform account connection.
keyPatterns: Bull queue for reliable scheduled dispatch
